brands.name: Tesla
brands.hint1: This is a modern automobile and clean energy brand.
brands.hint2: Known for launching cars into orbit and striving to make steering wheels optional through autonomy.
brands.hint3: In 2018, its founder famously tweeted about securing funding to go private at $420 a share, resulting in a hefty SEC fine.
brands.hint4: Its current eccentric and controversial CEO is Elon.

brands.name: Ferrari
brands.hint1: This is an ultra-luxury Italian automobile brand.
brands.hint2: Their speed demons wear a striking racing red and are adorned with a rearing black stallion.
brands.hint3: Originally founded solely to sponsor amateur drivers, they only built road-legal cars to fund their elite Formula 1 racing team.
brands.hint4: Its legendary founder was named Enzo.

brands.name: Toyota
brands.hint1: This is a massive Japanese automobile brand.
brands.hint2: Famous for a production system that eliminated waste and cars like the Hilux that are virtually indestructible.
brands.hint3: In the late 2000s, they faced a massive crisis and recalled millions of vehicles due to reports of unintended acceleration issues.
brands.hint4: The brand is named after its founding family, whose first leader was Kiichiro.

brands.name: Microsoft
brands.hint1: This is a pioneering software and personal computing giant.
brands.hint2: They conquered the office cubicle with a windowed interface and a suite of words, sheets, and slides.
brands.hint3: In the late 1990s, they were sued by the US government in a landmark antitrust trial for bundling their web browser with their operating system.
brands.hint4: Its most famous founder dropped out of Harvard and is named Bill.

brands.name: Google
brands.hint1: This is a dominant software, search, and advertising company.
brands.hint2: Their name represents an astronomically large number, and their primary mission is to organize all of human knowledge.
brands.hint3: They restructured under a holding parent company called Alphabet in 2015 and dropped their famous "Don't be evil" motto from their preface.
brands.hint4: Its current CEO, who grew up in India and studied at IIT Kharagpur, is Sundar.

brands.name: Adobe
brands.hint1: This is a creative software and digital media giant.
brands.hint2: They became the industry standard by letting people manipulate reality, turn fonts into vectors, and lock documents into uneditable formats.
brands.hint3: In 2023, they mutually terminated a blockbuster $20 billion acquisition of the design platform Figma due to intense regulatory scrutiny in Europe and the US.
brands.hint4: Its current long-standing CEO is Shantanu.

brands.name: Apple
brands.hint1: This is a premium consumer electronics and software brand.
brands.hint2: They designed a pocket-sized jukebox that evolved into a device that redefined how we touch, talk, and scroll.
brands.hint3: In 2017, they admitted to chemically throttling older phones' battery performance to prevent shutdowns, sparking a global scandal known as "Batterygate".
brands.hint4: Its legendary co-founder, who was famous for wearing a black turtleneck and blue jeans, was Steve.

brands.name: Sony
brands.hint1: This is a Japanese consumer electronics and entertainment giant.
brands.hint2: They put music in our pockets on cassette tape, and brought the ultimate gaming station into living rooms worldwide.
brands.hint3: In 2014, their movie studio subsidiary was hit by a devastating state-sponsored cyberattack that leaked unreleased films and embarrassing executive emails.
brands.hint4: One of its legendary co-founders, who wrote the book "Made in Japan", was Akio.

brands.name: Nintendo
brands.hint1: This is a historic Japanese electronics and video game brand.
brands.hint2: They started out making handmade playing cards in 1889 before saving the global video game industry with a heroic Italian plumber.
brands.hint3: Their hybrid home-handheld console faced class-action lawsuits over a persistent joystick drift defect in its detachable controllers.
brands.hint4: The legendary president who guided them from cards to video games was Hiroshi.

brands.name: Nike
brands.hint1: This is a major global athletic clothing and footwear brand.
brands.hint2: Their winged goddess emblem encourages millions with a simple three-word command to take action.
brands.hint3: In 2018, they made headlines by featuring controversial ex-NFL quarterback Colin Kaepernick in their 30th anniversary campaign.
brands.hint4: The co-founder who started selling shoes out of the trunk of his car is Phil.

brands.name: Zara
brands.hint1: This is a fast-fashion clothing retail giant.
brands.hint2: They mastered speed-to-market, turning a designer runway sketch into a store-shelf garment in under fifteen days.
brands.hint3: The brand faced boycotts and protests in late 2023 over an advertising campaign that critics claimed resembled imagery of the conflict in Gaza.
brands.hint4: Its secretive founder, who is one of the wealthiest self-made men in Europe, is Amancio.

brands.name: Patagonia
brands.hint1: This is an outdoor clothing and gear brand.
brands.hint2: They ran a famous black Friday ad telling customers "Don't Buy This Jacket" to promote recycling and sustainability.
brands.hint3: In 2022, the founder transferred 100% ownership of the multi-billion-dollar company to a trust and non-profit to fight climate change.
brands.hint4: Its eccentric rock-climbing founder is Yvon.

brands.name: Maybelline
brands.hint1: This is a mass-market cosmetics and makeup brand.
brands.hint2: They are famous for their lash-lengthening mascaras and a catchy jingle questioning if beauty is born or created.
brands.hint3: Founded in Chicago in 1915, the creator got the idea after watching his sister apply a mix of vaseline and coal dust to her eyelashes.
brands.hint4: The brand was named after the founder's sister, whose name was Mabel.

brands.name: MAC
brands.hint1: This is a premium, professional-grade cosmetics brand.
brands.hint2: Originally created as a makeup line specifically for photo shoots, they became legendary for their ultra-matte red lipstick named "Ruby Woo".
brands.hint3: They established a revolutionary AIDS fund in 1994, donating 100% of the selling price of their special lipstick line to support people living with HIV/AIDS.
brands.hint4: The brand was co-founded in Canada by a salon owner named Frank.

brands.name: Fenty Beauty
brands.hint1: This is a highly popular modern cosmetics brand.
brands.hint2: They disrupted the beauty industry by launching with a ground-breaking forty-shade foundation line, forcing competitors to become more inclusive.
brands.hint3: Their launch was named one of Time Magazine's best inventions of 2017 for its unprecedented inclusivity, starting the "Fenty Effect".
brands.hint4: The brand is founded and owned by the multi-platinum Barbadian singer Rihanna (whose last name is Fenty).

brands.name: Tiffany & Co.
brands.hint1: This is a luxury jewelry and silverware brand.
brands.hint2: Famous for a very specific, trademarked shade of robin's egg blue and an iconic breakfast scene outside their Manhattan flagship.
brands.hint3: In 2021, they were acquired by the luxury conglomerate LVMH for nearly $16 billion after a bitter legal battle over pricing during the pandemic.
brands.hint4: The company was founded in New York in 1837 by a young entrepreneur named Charles.

brands.name: Cartier
brands.hint1: This is a prestigious French jewelry and watch brand.
brands.hint2: Known as the "Jeweler of Kings" and famous for their sleek panther motif and screws-embellished "Love" bracelets.
brands.hint3: They invented one of the earliest practical wristwatches in 1904 for a Brazilian aviator who complained about using pocket watches while flying.
brands.hint4: The brand was founded in Paris in 1847 by Louis-Francois.

brands.name: Pandora
brands.hint1: This is a Danish mass-market jewelry brand.
brands.hint2: Famous for customizable charm bracelets that let customers carry their memories, milestones, and hobbies on their wrists.
brands.hint3: In 2021, they announced they would stop using mined diamonds in favor of laboratory-grown gems to improve ethical standards.
brands.hint4: It was founded as a small family jeweler in Copenhagen by Per.

brands.name: Ray-Ban
brands.hint1: This is a premium sunglasses and eyewear brand.
brands.hint2: They created anti-glare green lenses for US military aviators, which became a timeless pop-culture fashion symbol.
brands.hint3: Originally owned by Bausch & Lomb, they were sold in 1999 to the Italian eyewear giant Luxottica, which now dominates the global glasses market.
brands.hint4: They were originally commissioned in the 1930s by a US Army Air Corps Lieutenant General named John.

brands.name: Rolex
brands.hint1: This is an iconic Swiss luxury watch and accessory brand.
brands.hint2: Their emblem is a crown, and their mechanical chronometers are the ultimate global currency for status and achievement.
brands.hint3: They are owned by a private charitable trust (the Hans Wilsdorf Foundation), meaning they pay low taxes while donating massive sums to charity.
brands.hint4: The brand was founded in London in 1905 by Hans.

brands.name: Oakley
brands.hint1: This is an athletic performance eyewear and accessory brand.
brands.hint2: Known for futuristic, wrap-around sports sunglasses and protective eyewear used by top Olympians and military personnel.
brands.hint3: In 2010, they received worldwide press for providing free protective sunglasses to the thirty-three Chilean miners trapped underground for over two months.
brands.hint4: The founder started the company in his garage with $300, naming it after his dog, and his name is Jim.

brands.name: Hermes
brands.hint1: This is a ultra-luxury French leather goods and fashion brand.
brands.hint2: Their most famous leather handbags, named after a princess and an actress, are highly exclusive and require years on a waiting list.
brands.hint3: In 2023, they faced a lawsuit from consumers claiming they antitrust-bundled the sale of their Birkin bags by requiring customers to buy other accessories first.
brands.hint4: The brand was founded in Paris as an elite harness and saddle-making workshop by Thierry.

brands.name: Coach
brands.hint1: This is an American modern luxury leather goods brand.
brands.hint2: They started as a family workshop in a Manhattan loft making wallets inspired by the worn leather of a baseball glove.
brands.hint3: In 2021, they faced severe backlash after a TikTok video showed their slashed unsold handbags in dumpsters, violating their claims of sustainability.
brands.hint4: The brand was turned into a global powerhouse by a visionary leather craftsman named Miles.

brands.name: Gucci
brands.hint1: This is an Italian luxury leather and fashion house.
brands.hint2: Represented by a double-G monogram, they are famous for horsebit leather loafers and green-red-green web stripes.
brands.hint3: In the 1990s, the family-owned business collapsed into a tragic murder-for-hire plot, where the heir was assassinated by hitmen hired by his ex-wife.
brands.hint4: The brand was founded in Florence in 1921 by a former hotel porter named Guccio.

brands.name: Lego
brands.hint1: This is a world-famous toy and plastics brand.
brands.hint2: Their colorful interlocking ABS plastic bricks have built everything from tiny castles to full-size driveable sports cars.
brands.hint3: In 2023, they abandoned their efforts to make bricks from recycled PET bottles because the manufacturing process actually increased carbon emissions.
brands.hint4: The Danish carpenter who founded the company in 1932 was Ole.

brands.name: Tupperware
brands.hint1: This is a historic household plastics brand.
brands.hint2: They revolutionized food preservation with a patented airtight seal inspired by the lid of a paint can, sold via neighborhood social gatherings.
brands.hint3: In late 2024, they filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection after years of struggling with debt and failing to attract younger buyers.
brands.hint4: The company was founded by an American chemist who invented the seal, named Earl.

brands.name: Rubbermaid
brands.hint1: This is a large household and commercial plastics brand.
brands.hint2: They are famous for rugged plastic storage bins, heavy-duty trash cans, and durable food containers used in home and commercial kitchens.
brands.hint3: In 1999, the company was acquired by Newell in a multi-billion dollar merger to create a consumer goods giant, but struggled with integration.
brands.hint4: The brand was founded in Ohio in 1920, originally making rubber dustpans, by a businessman named James.

brands.name: L'Oreal
brands.hint1: This is the world's largest beauty and cosmetics conglomerate.
brands.hint2: They tell their customers that they are worth it, and dominate hair dye, skincare, and fragrances globally.
brands.hint3: For decades, their largest individual shareholder was Liliane Bettencourt, whose extreme wealth and family legal battles made constant headlines.
brands.hint4: The brand was founded in Paris in 1909 by a young chemist who developed a safe hair dye formula, named Eugene.

brands.name: Estee Lauder
brands.hint1: This is a high-end beauty and skincare company.
brands.hint2: They believe every woman can be beautiful and became famous by giving free gift-with-purchase samples to department store shoppers.
brands.hint3: They acquired luxury scent brand Le Labo and high-end makeup lines like Tom Ford Beauty, consolidating their grip on prestige beauty.
brands.hint4: The legendary female founder who built the empire from four basic skincare products was Estee.

brands.name: Sephora
brands.hint1: This is a global beauty and cosmetics retail giant.
brands.hint2: They revolutionized cosmetics shopping by letting customers touch, swatch, and try products freely on open-sell shelves instead of behind glass counters.
brands.hint3: Owned by the luxury conglomerate LVMH since 1997, they faced controversies over dynamic pricing and the recent phenomenon of "10-year-olds in Sephora" destroying product displays.
brands.hint4: The visionary French entrepreneur who created the open-sell retail concept in 1970 was Dominique.
